commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainers need to review the session-state timeout contract and contributor proof; there is no narrow automation repair to apply from this review.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue was found; the diff changes TypeScript compaction runtime logic and tests, with no dependency, workflow, secret, package, or external code-loading changes. (sha=407466404bc5f488924382fad9a13a7e48828eaf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves partial compaction summaries across timeout cancellation paths, surfaces timeout guidance in preflight failure copy, adds regression tests, and removes the now-unused node-host timeout helper.

PR surface: Source -40, Tests +26. Total -14 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main wraps compaction in one 180-second operation timeout and drops timeout partial progress before fallback can reuse it. I did not run a live slow-provider or gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to review the session-state timeout contract and contributor proof; there is no narrow automation repair to apply from this review.

target.title: fix compaction timeout partial progress
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve completed chunk summaries when a later compaction chunk times out, so timeout recovery can reuse partial progress instead of falling back to a generic “summary unavailable” note and restarting from chunk 1 on the next attempt.
- Preserve cooperative partial progress through the outer `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` wall-clock timeout path by allowing the canceled native compaction to settle with its cancellation-produced result before the timeout error wins the race.
- Wire the safety-timeout reason through the real native `activeSession.compact()` / `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` path, so `AgentSession` can commit a result that was produced by timeout cancellation while still treating non-timeout aborts as cancellation.
- Remove the old `src/node-host/with-timeout.ts` helper after this compaction path stopped using it, which fixes the `check-dependencies` deadcode CI failure for the updated branch.
- Keep caller abort cancellation semantics intact: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into successful partial summaries.
- Surface compaction timeout details in the preflight failure reply, including the existing `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` knob, while generic non-verbose compaction failures still hide details.

Fixes #92043

## Maintainer-ready notes

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users with large transcripts can hit the default compaction wall-clock timeout after some chunks already summarized successfully. Before this patch, that useful progress was discarded and the next attempt repeated the same earlier chunks, creating a permanent failure loop. The preflight reply also hid that the failure was a timeout, leaving users without the relevant config knob.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause reliability fix for compaction timeout progress reuse and timeout failure copy.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the local source/runtime surface covered here. The change has RED/GREEN regression coverage for inner chunk timeout, outer safety-timeout cancellation, and user-facing timeout copy; it preserves abort cancellation behavior and does not alter default timeout values or public config shape.
- **Root cause:** The root source was split across three timeout boundaries. `summarizeChunks()` discarded completed chunk summaries on timeout-shaped failures; the outer `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` could reject with its wall-clock timeout before a cooperating native compaction returned the partial result it produced during timeout cancellation; and the real native `AgentSession` path aborted compaction without preserving the timeout reason, so a result produced after timeout cancellation was still classified as plain aborted work.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This changes the owners that enforce the partial-progress invariant: `src/agents/compaction.ts` carries partial summaries from inner chunk failures, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts` preserves the timeout reason and prevents the outer timeout race from discarding a cancellation-produced result, and `src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts` commits a compact result produced by timeout cancellation instead of treating it as a user/request abort. The copy change stays at the preflight compaction failure formatter.
- **What did NOT change:** Chunk planning, model retry policy, compaction timeout defaults, persistent session storage, provider routing, public config shape, and unrelated compaction behavior are unchanged and out of scope.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `src/agents/compaction.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for summary partial-progress behavior, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts` owns the native compaction wall-clock race, and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for preflight agent-run failure copy.
- **Patch quality notes:** Timeout recovery is bounded to cooperative cancellation settlement at the timeout boundary. Non-timeout aborts still reject promptly, non-settling compactions still reject with `Compaction timed out`, and the default 180s timeout is unchanged.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Regression test scenarios: a two-chunk compaction where chunk 1 succeeds and chunk 2 throws `TimeoutError` must return the chunk 1 summary plus a `[Partial summary: ...]` marker; a compaction canceled by outer timeout and immediately resolving with progress must return that progress instead of `Compaction timed out`; direct native compaction passes the safety timeout reason into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` so timeout-aborted results can be committed; a preflight compaction timeout must surface the timeout reason and config guidance.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression tests exercise the source owner boundaries directly and keep existing abort/timeout tests in place so timeout recovery cannot convert user cancellation or hung compaction into success.
- **Out of scope:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, change the shape/default of `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds`, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.

## Root Cause

`src/agents/compaction.ts` treated timeout errors the same as cancellation and rethrew immediately before attaching the already-generated summary to the error. `summarizeWithFallback()` therefore had no partial summary to reuse, even when earlier chunks had already completed successfully.

The outer safety timeout added a second loss path: `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` raced native compaction against a timeout rejection. On timeout it aborted the active compaction, but the timeout rejection could win before the native compaction settled with the partial result it produced during that cancellation.

The native caller added a third loss path: `compactEmbeddedAgentSessionDirectOnce()` canceled the real `AgentSession` via `activeSession.abortCompaction()` with no timeout reason, and `AgentSession.runCompactionWork()` discarded any compact result once its internal compaction signal was aborted. Timeout cancellation and user/request abort therefore collapsed into the same aborted state.

Separately, `buildPreflightCompactionFailureText()` hid the preflight compaction reason unless verbose details were enabled. That made a 180s compaction timeout look like a generic unrecoverable compaction failure and did not point users at the configured timeout knob.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Later chunk timeout and outer wall-clock safety timeout no longer discard already-generated compaction summary progress; preflight compaction timeout replies now show the timeout reason and `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` guidance.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043`, branch `feat/issue-92043`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts -t "passes the timeout reason to onCancel|aborts early on external abort"
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts -t "passes safety timeout cancellation reason into native compaction abort"
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 pnpm deadcode:dependencies && OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 pnpm deadcode:unused-files && OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 pnpm deadcode:report:ci:ts-unused
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.harness.ts src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts
```

```bash
git -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  4 passed | 42 skipped (46)
```

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 138 skipped (140)
```

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  101 passed (101)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 22.86s
```

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit exited 0 with no stdout/stderr output.
```

```text
$ pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 dlx --package knip@6.8.0 knip --config config/knip.config.ts --production --no-progress --reporter compact --dependencies --no-config-hints
$ node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs
[deadcode] Knip unused-file allowlist matched 33 intentional entries.
$ mkdir -p .artifacts/deadcode && pnpm deadcode:ts-unused > .artifacts/deadcode/ts-unused-exports.txt 2>&1 || true
```

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 65ms on 6 files using 1 threads.
```

```text
git diff --check exited 0 with no whitespace errors.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** A later timeout after chunk 1 completes returns the chunk 1 summary with a `[Partial summary: chunks 1-1 of 2 were summarized...]` marker instead of the generic unavailable summary. The outer safety-timeout regression resolves with `partial summary after chunk 1` when the timeout abort signal causes the compaction to settle with progress. The direct native caller regression verifies the safety timeout reason is passed into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)`, and `AgentSession` preserves a compact result only for that timeout reason while non-timeout aborts still reject as aborts. The dependency deadcode shard passes after removing the now-unused old timeout helper. Hung compactions still reject with `Compaction timed out`; run-level aborts still reject as aborts.
- **What was not tested:** No live external model provider, long-running 180s wall-clock gateway run, Desktop session, or external channel transport was exercised. The summary-progress regression is covered through production compaction fallback code with a mocked summarizer timeout seam and through the production safety-timeout wrapper with fake timers.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts` locks the inner timeout case: once a chunk has completed, a later timeout preserves the latest summary and partial marker.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts` locks the outer safety-timeout race: timeout cancellation can return cooperative partial progress before the timeout error wins, and timeout/external abort reasons are threaded into the cancel hook.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts` locks the real direct native caller: safety timeout cancellation passes the timeout reason into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` before the native compact result is returned.
- The existing abort and hung-timeout tests remain green: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into partial-success summaries, and non-settling compactions still reject with timeout.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state, reliability, user-facing copy, timeout handling.
- **Risk explanation:** The behavior change is limited to compaction timeout errors after useful progress exists or when a cooperating compaction settles because of timeout cancellation. First-chunk failures still fall through to the existing generic fallback, hung compactions still time out, and non-timeout aborts still propagate as cancellation.
- **Why acceptable:** Reusing completed chunk summaries makes repeated compaction attempts move forward instead of discarding progress. The copy change only reveals timeout reasons for compaction timeout failures; generic non-verbose compaction errors remain redacted.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.
target.author: mushuiyu886
